usage: legalizePin [-help] [-auto_pairing] [-ignore {pin_spacing pin_spacing_constraint pin_spacing_routeBlk pin_width pin_depth pin_layer pin_min_area pin_on_track pin_abutment pin_non_nbr_abutment clones pin_color}] [-internalPin] [-keepLayer] [-keepOrder] [-moveFixedPin] [-pin {pinName | pinNameList}] [-snapToBoundary] [-verbose] [ -ptn <string> ] [-ptn <string> | {-pin_file <fileName> [-exclude_ptn <string>]} | -exclude_pin_file <fileName>]

options.name: -auto_pairing
options.description: Command calculates the channel width or distance between pins which will be considered for pairing

options.name: -exclude_pin_file
options.description: Specify pin-list to be excluded for partition in a file

options.name: -exclude_ptn
options.description: Specifies the list of exluded partition for pin-file

options.name: -ignore
options.description: {pin_spacing pin_spacing_constraint pin_spacing_routeBlk pin_width pin_depth pin_layer pin_min_area pin_on_track pin_abutment pin_non_nbr_abutment clones pin_color} Legalize taking the category(ies) of ignore types into account.

options.name: -internalPin
options.description: Legalizes internal pins by moving them to the closest track on the preferred routing layer.

options.name: -keepLayer
options.description: Maintains the pin layer while legalizing the pins.

options.name: -keepOrder
options.description: Maintains the pin order while legalizing pins irrespective of the layers.

options.name: -moveFixedPin
options.description: If you use this parameter, the specified pins with a Fixed status can also be moved.

options.name: -pin
options.description: Specifies that the command should be run on the specified pin(s) only.

options.name: -pin_file
options.description: Specify pin-list for partition in a file

options.name: -ptn
options.description: Specifies the partition for which the pins will be checked.

options.name: -snapToBoundary
options.description: Snaps internal partition pin(s) to its boundary.

options.name: -verbose
options.description: Reports all the existing pin violations.
